message:
Add tuple support, string methods, and print improvements

Agent 1: Print improvements for PyObjects and subscripts
- Fixed print() to unwrap PyObjects from enumerate/zip
- Added switch-based unwrapping for subscript results (list[i], dict[key])

Agent 2: Complete tuple implementation
- Added Tuple AST node type
- Implemented tuple parsing (1, 2, 3)
- Added tuple unpacking (a, b = tuple)
- Added tuple subscripting tuple[0]
- Added PyTuple.print() for formatted output

Agent 3: String method calls and concatenation
- Added string concatenation operator (+)
- Fixed string method routing (upper, lower, split, replace, strip)
- Added type tracking for concat results

Files modified:
- src/ast.zig: Added Tuple node type
- src/parser.zig: Tuple parsing and unpacking
- src/codegen/*: Tuple codegen, string operators, print improvements
- packages/runtime/src/pytuple.zig: Tuple printing

Test Status: 14-15/75 passing
Known Issue: Compiler memory leaks (to be fixed next)

files.patch:
@@ -1,9 +1,7 @@
-# Basic tuple creation and access
-pair = (1, 2)
-print(pair[0])
-print(pair[1])
-
-triple = (10, 20, 30)
-print(triple[0])
-print(triple[1])
-print(triple[2])
+# Tuple basics - creation, indexing, printing
+t = (1, 2, 3)
+print(t)
+print(t[0])
+print(t[1])
+print(t[2])
+print(len(t))

@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+# Tuple unpacking - from literal and from variable
+a, b = (10, 20)
+print(a)
+print(b)
+
+t = (1, 2, 3)
+x, y, z = t
+print(x)
+print(y)
+print(z)
